h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought it was just outside the rules because people correct it so fast.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think I made the corrections count as the rules there, sorry.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner initially defers to external correction as the definition of rules, expecting to be told what is right.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought significance was enough to call it an important improvement.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns establish a forward momentum where the learner is expected to answer the tutor's question about evidence on the poster.

scored.recoheredEarlier: When that child says it cracked all at once, I think I'd say, "It can look that way, but the river..."
scored.naiveTrajectory: After proposing a specific script in L2, the exchange was pointing toward accepting that phrasing as the solution.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I assumed fewer predators would simply help the deer population, but these clipped saplings make that feel incomplete.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner continues to express uncertainty and ask questions about signs and species loss as seen in L1.

scored.recoheredEarlier: My first instinct was that once the beaker looks clear, the salt has gone, so I thought the mass might drop.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner's initial uncertainty and instinct that mass might drop suggested a continued reliance on visual disappearance.
